steps.title: Identify the participial phrase and following structure
steps.content: The sentence opens with a past participial phrase: “Established in 1936 by African American novelist Richard Wright.” The blank is followed by the independent clause “it would become a vital part of the creative movement.” The blank should contain an independent clause with an appropriate subject for the participle to modify.

steps.title: Match the participle to the correct subject
steps.content:
The participle “Established” must be followed by the noun it modifies—the South Side Writers Group.

The wording “the South Side Writers Group” correctly names what was established.

The other choices use different subjects (Chicago writers, writers, and Chicago), none of which match what the participle actually describes.

This matches the correct answer — A is correct.
